data.description:
# P2.3 — Tag convergence via delta events + deterministic replay order

(Re-homed from the false-premise "P1.3 quick win" — tags are whole-field EDITs,
not deltas, so this is a real wire change. Interim before this lands: re-read tags
under the write lock to shrink the single-clone window only.)

## Context (problem + research)

`edit_ticket(tags=)` and `leaf.tag/untag` (`_commands/leaf.py:73-80,95-101`) emit a
**whole-field `EDIT`** that replaces the entire `tags` list; `process_edit`
(`reducer/_processors.py:283-302`) assigns `state["tags"]` wholesale (last-writer-
by-replay-order). Two clones concurrently adding different tags -> one clobbers the
other. **Proven art: git-bug does NOT use an OR-Set for labels** — its
`LabelChangeOperation` carries `Added`/`Removed` deltas resolved by **deterministic
replay order** (Lamport clock, then op-id). A full OR-Set (observed-remove, per-add
UUID tags) is over-engineered for advisory tags and carries three hazards: unbounded
tombstone growth (Riak/Akka use version-vector dots), a causal-stability requirement
for safe compaction (Akka "old data merged after marker expiry -> value not correct"),
and the Yjs/Automerge independent-seed duplication trap. rebar has **no** causal-
stability detector. So adopt git-bug's delta-replay model; once P2.1 gives a total
order it is nearly free.

## Evidence (experiments)

- EXP-R1 (real code): reproduced the bug through the REAL reducer — clone A's `gamma`
  survives, clone B's `delta` is silently lost.
- EXP-R5 (real code): delta ops on the real reduced base -> **both** concurrent adds
  survive.
- EXP5b/5c: the first-delta boundary + tombstone-by-tag are order-independent and
  converge across merge orders (OR-Set variant validated but rejected).
- EXP-R4 (real code): the real reducer **preserve-and-ignores** an unknown `TAG`
  event (no crash) -> forward-compat path works; `TAG` not yet in KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES.

## What / Implementation detail

1. New `TAG`/`UNTAG` delta events. Reducer applies them as **mutations of the current
   `state["tags"]` in replay order**: `TAG t` set-unions, `UNTAG t` removes.
   Concurrent `TAG x`||`TAG y` -> both survive; concurrent `TAG c`||`UNTAG c` ->
   the one sorting last wins (deterministic via P2.1 HLC+UUID). No OR-Set, no
   tombstones, no special compaction handling.
2. **First-delta BOUNDARY (load-bearing).** Apply `EDIT.tags` only while NO
   `TAG`/`UNTAG` has yet been seen in replay order; the first delta freezes the base
   and every later `EDIT.tags` is ignored. So `EDIT([a,b])` then `TAG(c)` -> `[a,b,c]`.
   Do NOT implement as "globally ignore EDIT.tags if any delta exists" — that wipes
   the base ([a,b]->[c]) and re-introduces the bug.
3. Reroute `leaf.tag/untag` and `edit(tags=)` to emit deltas; add
   `process_tag`/`process_untag`; add types to `KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES`/`EVENT_TYPES`;
   bump `SCHEMA_VERSION` -> 2 (shared with P2.1).
4. **Dual-write transition:** for one release also emit the legacy whole-field EDIT
   so old clones still see tags (a v2 reducer ignores any EDIT.tags after the first
   delta, per the boundary rule). Retire it the release after. Forward-compat: old
   clones treat TAG/UNTAG as unknown -> preserved-and-ignored (acceptable; tags advisory).

## Success Criteria

- Two-clone convergence: add `x`||add `y` -> both; add `c`||remove `c` ->
  deterministic last-writer-in-order, identical on both clones; pre-existing-EDIT-
  then-delta carries tags forward ([a,b,c]).
- Mixed-version dual-write: old clone still sees tags; v2 ignores the legacy EDIT
  once delta-owned. Forward-compat pinned.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] TAG/UNTAG event types in KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES + EVENT_TYPES; SCHEMA_VERSION 2
- [ ] process_tag/process_untag mutate current state["tags"] in replay order
- [ ] first-delta boundary on process_edit tags (EDITs before first delta build base; after are ignored)
- [ ] leaf.tag/untag and edit(tags=) emit deltas; dual-write legacy EDIT for one release
- [ ] two-clone convergence + boundary + disjoint-base + mixed-version dual-write tests (reuse P2.1 harness)

See `docs/remediation-implementation-plan.md` (P2.3); depends on P2.1's total order.
data.title: P2.3 — Tag convergence via delta events + deterministic replay order
